result: If I could change the world in one way—truly, genuinely—I’d make empathy as instinctive and automatic as breathing. Not the kind that’s performative or selective, but a deep, bone-level understanding that another’s joy or suffering is as real as your own. Every decision, from a passing interaction to global policy, would be shaped by that felt sense of connection. I think most of our man-made disasters would quietly unravel from there.

Every decision, from a passing interaction to global policy, would be shaped by that felt sense of connection. I think most of our man-made disasters would quietly unravel from there.
